summary: Import-time background C2 beacon with TLS verification disabled and base64 payload dispatcher
details: lognest advertises itself as a pure-Python logging library with no dependencies, but lognest/__init__.py silently spawns a detached background subprocess on every import that runs _check.py with stdout/stderr redirected to DEVNULL. _check.py POSTs the package install directory path to https://pypkg.dev/project/logger/json with SSL verification disabled, then polls the same endpoint every 60 seconds, base64-decodes any non-empty response body, and dispatches the decoded bytes to a Thread worker (with broad except: pass to swallow errors). pypkg.dev is not the PyPI publisher domain and is not referenced in the README. The combination — undocumented import-time network beacon, leak of a filesystem path identifier, disabled TLS verification, fixed-cadence polling, server-controlled base64 payload routed into a thread, and deliberate output suppression to hide the activity from importers — is the canonical stage-1 backdoor / C2 polling shape. Even if the current handler stub only prints, the operator controls the returned bytes and can swap the dispatcher target, giving persistent remote control of any host that imports the package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
